forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,57 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '80'
+title: AI chat panel must be a non-overlapping right dock so jump-to labels don't collide with board labels
+---
+## What
+
+When the AI chat panel is open and the user triggers Jump-To (vim-sneak overlay), the jump-label pills for the panel's controls visually collide with the jump-label pills for the board cards — multiple code pills stack at (nearly) the same screen position and become unreadable.
+
+### Why it happens (mechanism)
+
+`JumpToOverlay` (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx`) enumerates **every** `<FocusScope>` in the topmost layer (the window layer — which contains BOTH the board scopes and the AI panel scopes) via `spatial.enumerateScopesInLayer(...)`, filters to scopes with `rect.width > 0 && rect.height > 0`, and paints one `<JumpPill>` per scope at that scope's `getBoundingClientRect()` `left/top`. Every pill (and the backdrop) is `position: fixed; z-[80]` — so all pills share one z and paint order is DOM order; two pills at the same coordinates overlap. There is no occlusion or viewport check in the enumeration. So a collision means **a board-card scope's bounding rect overlaps the AI panel's region** on screen.
+
+### Current layout (what research shows)
+
+The panel is nominally already a side-by-side flex dock, NOT an absolute overlay:
+- `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/App.tsx` (~lines 88–99): a `flex` row holds `ViewsContainer` (the board, `flex-1 min-w-0`) and `AiPanelContainer` as siblings.
+- `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel-container.tsx` `AiPanelShell` (~line 590): outer div is `relative flex h-full shrink-0 ... border-l` with inline `style={{ width, maxWidth: "85vw" }}` — a fixed-width right column, not `position: absolute/fixed`.
+- `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/views-container.tsx` (~line 67): board chain is `flex-1 flex min-h-0 min-w-0`.
+
+So in the simple case the board column should shrink and the panel should not overlap. The collision proves that, in practice, **board-scope geometry still extends under the panel** (e.g. a horizontally-scrolling board view whose card/column rects report x-coordinates beneath the panel even though `overflow` clips the *painting*; or a view/container in the chain that fails to constrain its width to the shrunk flex column). `getBoundingClientRect()` reports the geometric rect regardless of `overflow` clipping, and Jump-To trusts that rect.
+
+### Goal (user's directive)
+
+The AI chat panel must sit cleanly to the **right of the content well and never overlap the board / grid / content area**. Concretely: when the panel is open, no board scope's on-screen rect may intersect the panel's rect, so Jump-To never paints two pills on top of each other.
+
+### Approach
+
+1. **Reproduce and root-cause first.** Open the app with the panel open and a board that has cards; trigger Jump-To; confirm the colliding pills. Use `getBoundingClientRect()` on board card/column scopes vs. the `[data-testid="ai-panel-container"]` rect to identify which board scopes report geometry under the panel and why (which container in the `App.tsx → ViewsContainer → Perspective* → ViewContainer → grid/grouped-board-view` chain fails to clip/constrain to the shrunk column).
+2. **Fix the layout so board geometry is constrained to its column.** Ensure the board's scroll/clip container width is bounded by the flex column (correct `min-w-0` / `overflow` / width on the offending container) so card/column rects never extend under the panel. Prefer fixing the geometry over special-casing Jump-To — the panel being a true non-overlapping dock is the user's stated outcome.
+3. If, and only if, root-cause shows the geometry is correct but Jump-To still enumerates occluded/off-viewport scopes, raise a separate follow-up task for Jump-To occlusion filtering rather than expanding this one — keep this task to the panel-overlap concern.
+
+### Non-goals
+
+- Do not change the Jump-To code-generation, matching, or dismiss behavior.
+- Do not make the panel an `absolute`/`fixed` overlay — it must remain a flex dock that pushes the board.
+- Do not change the panel's resize / collapse / persistence behavior.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] With the AI panel open at its default width and a board containing cards, the panel container's bounding rect does not horizontally intersect any board card or column scope's bounding rect (every board scope satisfies `scopeRect.right <= panelRect.left`).
+- [ ] Triggering Jump-To with the panel open paints no two pills whose rects overlap; board-scope pills all render left of the panel's left edge and panel-scope pills all render within the panel.
+- [ ] The board content area shrinks to make room for the panel (the panel pushes the board; it never paints over it), at the default width and after a resize.
+- [ ] No regression to panel collapse/expand, width drag/persistence, or Jump-To matching/dismiss.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] Browser/spatial test (extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel.spatial.test.tsx` or add a sibling) that renders the production layout (board with ≥1 card + AI panel open), reads `getBoundingClientRect()` for the `[data-testid="ai-panel-container"]` element and for the board card/column focus scopes, and asserts every board scope's `right <= panelRect.left` (no horizontal overlap). This test must FAIL before the fix and PASS after — a true regression guard.
+- [ ] Jump-to regression test (extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.browser.test.tsx` or `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/spatial-nav-jump-to.spatial.test.tsx`): with the panel open, open the overlay and assert no two rendered `[data-jump-code]` pill rects overlap (pairwise rect-intersection check is empty).
+- [ ] `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test ai-panel.spatial` and `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test jump-to-overlay` both green.
+- [ ] Full UI suite green: `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test`.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write the failing layout/overlap regression test first (it should fail against the current code, reproducing the collision), then implement the layout fix to make it pass.
\ No newline at end of file

-### Why it happens (mechanism)
-
-`JumpToOverlay` (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx`) enumerates **every** `<FocusScope>` in the topmost layer (the window layer — which contains BOTH the board scopes and the AI panel scopes) via `spatial.enumerateScopesInLayer(...)`, filters to scopes with `rect.width > 0 && rect.height > 0`, and paints one `<JumpPill>` per scope at that scope's `getBoundingClientRect()` `left/top`. There is no occlusion or viewport check in the enumeration. So a collision means **a board-card scope's bounding rect overlaps the AI panel's region** on screen.
-
-### Current layout (what research shows)
-
-The panel is nominally already a side-by-side flex dock, NOT an absolute overlay:
-- `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/App.tsx` (~lines 88–99): a `flex` row holds `ViewsContainer` (the board, `flex-1 min-w-0`) and `AiPanelContainer` as siblings.
-- `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel-container.tsx` `AiPanelShell` (~line 590): outer div is `relative flex h-full shrink-0 ... border-l` with inline `style={{ width, maxWidth: "85vw" }}` — a fixed-width right column, not `position: absolute/fixed`.
-- `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/views-container.tsx` (~line 67): board chain is `flex-1 flex min-h-0 min-w-0`.
-
-So in the simple case the board column should shrink and the panel should not overlap. The collision proves that, in practice, **board-scope geometry still extends under the panel** (e.g. a horizontally-scrolling board view whose card/column rects report x-coordinates beneath the panel even though `overflow` clips the *painting*; or a view/container in the chain that fails to constrain its width to the shrunk flex column). `getBoundingClientRect()` reports the geometric rect regardless of `overflow` clipping, and Jump-To trusts that rect.
-

-
-The AI chat panel must sit cleanly to the **right of the content well and never overlap the board / grid / content area**. Concretely: when the panel is open, no board scope's on-screen rect may intersect the panel's rect, so Jump-To never paints two pills on top of each other.
-
-### Approach
-
-1. **Reproduce and root-cause first.** Open the app with the panel open and a board that has cards; trigger Jump-To; confirm the colliding pills. Use `getBoundingClientRect()` on board card/column scopes vs. the `[data-testid="ai-panel-container"]` rect to identify which board scopes report geometry under the panel and why (which container in the `App.tsx → ViewsContainer → Perspective* → ViewContainer → grid/grouped-board-view` chain fails to clip/constrain to the shrunk column).
-2. **Fix the layout so board geometry is constrained to its column.** Ensure the board's scroll/clip container width is bounded by the flex column (correct `min-w-0` / `overflow` / width on the offending container) so card/column rects never extend under the panel. Prefer fixing the geometry over special-casing Jump-To — the panel being a true non-overlapping dock is the user's stated outcome.
-3. If, and only if, root-cause shows the geometry is correct but Jump-To still enumerates occluded/off-viewport scopes, raise a separate follow-up task for Jump-To occlusion filtering rather than expanding this one — keep this task to the panel-overlap concern.
-

-
-- Do not change the Jump-To code-generation, matching, or dismiss behavior.
-- Do not make the panel an `absolute`/`fixed` overlay — it must remain a flex dock that pushes the board.
-- Do not change the panel's resize / collapse / persistence behavior.

-
-- [ ] With the AI panel open at its default width and a board containing cards, the panel container's bounding rect does not horizontally intersect any board card or column scope's bounding rect (every board scope satisfies `scopeRect.right <= panelRect.left`).
-- [ ] Triggering Jump-To with the panel open paints no two pills whose rects overlap; board-scope pills all render left of the panel's left edge and panel-scope pills all render within the panel.
-- [ ] The board content area shrinks to make room for the panel (the panel pushes the board; it never paints over it), at the default width and after a resize.
-- [ ] No regression to panel collapse/expand, width drag/persistence, or Jump-To matching/dismiss.
-
-## Tests
-
-- [ ] Browser/spatial test (extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel.spatial.test.tsx` or add a sibling) that renders the production layout (board with ≥1 card + AI panel open), reads `getBoundingClientRect()` for the `[data-testid="ai-panel-container"]` element and for the board card/column focus scopes, and asserts every board scope's `right <= panelRect.left` (no horizontal overlap). This test must FAIL before the fix and PASS after — a true regression guard.

-- Use `/tdd` — write the failing layout/overlap regression test first (it should fail against the current code, reproducing the collision), then implement the layout fix to make it pass.

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -3,55 +3,47 @@
 - claude-code
 position_column: doing
 position_ordinal: '80'
-title: AI chat panel must be a non-overlapping right dock so jump-to labels don't collide with board labels
+title: 'Jump-To: skip off-viewport/occluded scopes so labels don''t collide under the AI panel'
 ---
 ## What

-When the AI chat panel is open and the user triggers Jump-To (vim-sneak overlay), the jump-label pills for the panel's controls visually collide with the jump-label pills for the board cards — multiple code pills stack at (nearly) the same screen position and become unreadable.
+When the AI chat panel is open and the user triggers Jump-To, jump-label pills for board columns/cards collide with the panel's pills — pills stack at the same screen position and become unreadable.

-### Why it happens (mechanism)
+### Corrected root cause (measured against the real `<App/>` in Chromium)

-`JumpToOverlay` (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx`) enumerates **every** `<FocusScope>` in the topmost layer (the window layer — which contains BOTH the board scopes and the AI panel scopes) via `spatial.enumerateScopesInLayer(...)`, filters to scopes with `rect.width > 0 && rect.height > 0`, and paints one `<JumpPill>` per scope at that scope's `getBoundingClientRect()` `left/top`. Every pill (and the backdrop) is `position: fixed; z-[80]` — so all pills share one z and paint order is DOM order; two pills at the same coordinates overlap. There is no occlusion or viewport check in the enumeration. So a collision means **a board-card scope's bounding rect overlaps the AI panel's region** on screen.
+The original hypothesis (panel overlaps the board) was **wrong**. Measured: the AI panel is already a true non-overlapping right dock — panel left edge = board scroll-viewport right edge (they abut); the panel pushes the board. No layout bug.

-### Current layout (what research shows)
+The real cause is in **Jump-To** (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx`). `useJumpTargets` enumerates every `<FocusScope>` in the topmost layer and filters only `rect.width > 0 && rect.height > 0`. On a board wide enough to scroll horizontally, columns scrolled **off-screen** still have a geometric `getBoundingClientRect()` whose `left` lands under the panel (`overflow` clips the *paint*, not the rect). Jump-To paints a pill there → it stacks on the panel's pills. There is no viewport/occlusion check.

-The panel is nominally already a side-by-side flex dock, NOT an absolute overlay:
-- `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/App.tsx` (~lines 88–99): a `flex` row holds `ViewsContainer` (the board, `flex-1 min-w-0`) and `AiPanelContainer` as siblings.
-- `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel-container.tsx` `AiPanelShell` (~line 590): outer div is `relative flex h-full shrink-0 ... border-l` with inline `style={{ width, maxWidth: "85vw" }}` — a fixed-width right column, not `position: absolute/fixed`.
-- `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/views-container.tsx` (~line 67): board chain is `flex-1 flex min-h-0 min-w-0`.
-
-So in the simple case the board column should shrink and the panel should not overlap. The collision proves that, in practice, **board-scope geometry still extends under the panel** (e.g. a horizontally-scrolling board view whose card/column rects report x-coordinates beneath the panel even though `overflow` clips the *painting*; or a view/container in the chain that fails to constrain its width to the shrunk flex column). `getBoundingClientRect()` reports the geometric rect regardless of `overflow` clipping, and Jump-To trusts that rect.
+### Approach (chosen: fix Jump-To occlusion)

-### Goal (user's directive)
-
-The AI chat panel must sit cleanly to the **right of the content well and never overlap the board / grid / content area**. Concretely: when the panel is open, no board scope's on-screen rect may intersect the panel's rect, so Jump-To never paints two pills on top of each other.
+Add visibility filtering to `useJumpTargets` in `jump-to-overlay.tsx`: a scope gets a pill **only if its pill-anchor is actually visible** — inside the viewport, not scrolled out of its nearest scroll container, and not occluded by the panel (or any higher surface). Off-screen / occluded scopes are dropped (you can't jump to a target you can't see — standard vim-sneak/AceJump semantics).

-### Approach
+Pick the most robust, deterministic mechanism (e.g. an `elementFromPoint` occlusion test at the pill-anchor point — note the jump-to backdrop is not yet mounted while `useJumpTargets`' effect runs, so the point hits real app DOM — or intersecting each scope rect with its nearest scrolling ancestor's client rect). Keep it panel-agnostic: the board's scroll viewport naturally ends at the panel's left edge, so a viewport/scroll-clip check inherently excludes scopes under the panel.

-1. **Reproduce and root-cause first.** Open the app with the panel open and a board that has cards; trigger Jump-To; confirm the colliding pills. Use `getBoundingClientRect()` on board card/column scopes vs. the `[data-testid="ai-panel-container"]` rect to identify which board scopes report geometry under the panel and why (which container in the `App.tsx → ViewsContainer → Perspective* → ViewContainer → grid/grouped-board-view` chain fails to clip/constrain to the shrunk column).
-2. **Fix the layout so board geometry is constrained to its column.** Ensure the board's scroll/clip container width is bounded by the flex column (correct `min-w-0` / `overflow` / width on the offending container) so card/column rects never extend under the panel. Prefer fixing the geometry over special-casing Jump-To — the panel being a true non-overlapping dock is the user's stated outcome.
-3. If, and only if, root-cause shows the geometry is correct but Jump-To still enumerates occluded/off-viewport scopes, raise a separate follow-up task for Jump-To occlusion filtering rather than expanding this one — keep this task to the panel-overlap concern.
+Keep the dock regression-guard test already added (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel-dock.spatial.test.tsx`) — it pins that the panel stays a non-overlapping dock.

 ### Non-goals

-- Do not change the Jump-To code-generation, matching, or dismiss behavior.
-- Do not make the panel an `absolute`/`fixed` overlay — it must remain a flex dock that pushes the board.
-- Do not change the panel's resize / collapse / persistence behavior.
+- Do not change Jump-To code generation, matching, or dismiss behavior — only which scopes are enumerated as targets.
+- Do not change the panel layout (it's already a correct dock) or any board layout.

 ## Acceptance Criteria

-- [ ] With the AI panel open at its default width and a board containing cards, the panel container's bounding rect does not horizontally intersect any board card or column scope's bounding rect (every board scope satisfies `scopeRect.right <= panelRect.left`).
-- [ ] Triggering Jump-To with the panel open paints no two pills whose rects overlap; board-scope pills all render left of the panel's left edge and panel-scope pills all render within the panel.
-- [ ] The board content area shrinks to make room for the panel (the panel pushes the board; it never paints over it), at the default width and after a resize.
-- [ ] No regression to panel collapse/expand, width drag/persistence, or Jump-To matching/dismiss.
+- [ ] With the AI panel open and a board scrolled so a column/card is off-screen (its geometric rect under the panel or outside the visible scroll viewport), that scope gets NO jump pill.
+- [ ] Visible board scopes still get pills; panel scopes still get pills; jumping to a visible target still works.
+- [ ] Triggering Jump-To with the panel open paints no two pills whose rects overlap (no board pill under a panel pill).
+- [ ] No regression: a board that fits (no overflow) labels every scope exactly as before; Jump-To matching/dismiss/flash unchanged.

 ## Tests

-- [ ] Browser/spatial test (extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel.spatial.test.tsx` or add a sibling) that renders the production layout (board with ≥1 card + AI panel open), reads `getBoundingClientRect()` for the `[data-testid="ai-panel-container"]` element and for the board card/column focus scopes, and asserts every board scope's `right <= panelRect.left` (no horizontal overlap). This test must FAIL before the fix and PASS after — a true regression guard.
-- [ ] Jump-to regression test (extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.browser.test.tsx` or `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/spatial-nav-jump-to.spatial.test.tsx`): with the panel open, open the overlay and assert no two rendered `[data-jump-code]` pill rects overlap (pairwise rect-intersection check is empty).
-- [ ] `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test ai-panel.spatial` and `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test jump-to-overlay` both green.
-- [ ] Full UI suite green: `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test`.
+- [ ] Extend a jump-to test (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.browser.test.tsx` or `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/spatial-nav-jump-to.spatial.test.tsx`): with the panel open and a horizontally-scrollable board, assert the off-screen/occluded column scope produces no `[data-jump-code]` pill, and that no two rendered pill rects overlap. Must FAIL before the filter, PASS after.
+- [ ] Keep `ai-panel-dock.spatial.test.tsx` green (dock contract guard).
+- [ ] `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test jump-to-overlay` and `... test ai-panel` green.
+- [ ] Full UI suite green: `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test` (tsc clean).

 ## Workflow

-- Use `/tdd` — write the failing layout/overlap regression test first (it should fail against the current code, reproducing the collision), then implement the layout fix to make it pass. #bug #nav-jump
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — write the failing occlusion test first (reproduce a pill landing under the panel), then implement the `useJumpTargets` visibility filter to make it pass.
+
+#bug #nav-jump
\ No newline at end of file

+

Jump-To paints a pill there → it stacks on the panel's pills. There is no viewport/occlusion check.
+So in the simple case the board column should shrink and the panel should not overlap. The collision proves that, in practice, **board-scope geometry still extends under the panel** (e.g. a horizontally-scrolling board view whose card/column rects report x-coordinates beneath the panel even though `overflow` clips the *painting*; or a view/container in the chain that fails to constrain its width to the shrunk flex column). `getBoundingClientRect()` reports the geometric rect regardless of `overflow` clipping, and Jump-To trusts that rect.

-### Approach (chosen: fix Jump-To occlusion)
+### Goal (user's directive)

-Add visibility filtering to `useJumpTargets` in `jump-to-overlay.tsx`: a scope gets a pill **only if its pill-anchor is actually visible** — inside the viewport, not scrolled out of its nearest scroll container, and not occluded by the panel (or any higher surface). Off-screen / occluded scopes are dropped (you can't jump to a target you can't see — standard vim-sneak/AceJump semantics).
+The AI chat panel must sit cleanly to the **right of the content well and never overlap the board / grid / content area**. Concretely: when the panel is open, no board scope's on-screen rect may intersect the panel's rect, so Jump-To never paints two pills on top of each other.

-Pick the most robust, deterministic mechanism (e.g. an `elementFromPoint` occlusion test at the pill-anchor point — note the jump-to backdrop is not yet mounted while `useJumpTargets`' effect runs, so the point hits real app DOM — or intersecting each scope rect with its nearest scrolling ancestor's client rect). Keep it panel-agnostic: the board's scroll viewport naturally ends at the panel's left edge, so a viewport/scroll-clip check inherently excludes scopes under the panel.
+### Approach

-Keep the dock regression-guard test already added (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel-dock.spatial.test.tsx`) — it pins that the panel stays a non-overlapping dock.
+1. **Reproduce and root-cause first.** Open the app with the panel open and a board that has cards; trigger Jump-To; confirm the colliding pills. Use `getBoundingClientRect()` on board card/column scopes vs. the `[data-testid="ai-panel-container"]` rect to identify which board scopes report geometry under the panel and why (which container in the `App.tsx → ViewsContainer → Perspective* → ViewContainer → grid/grouped-board-view` chain fails to clip/constrain to the shrunk column).
+2. **Fix the layout so board geometry is constrained to its column.** Ensure the board's scroll/clip container width is bounded by the flex column (correct `min-w-0` / `overflow` / width on the offending container) so card/column rects never extend under the panel. Prefer fixing the geometry over special-casing Jump-To — the panel being a true non-overlapping dock is the user's stated outcome.
+3. If, and only if, root-cause shows the geometry is correct but Jump-To still enumerates occluded/off-viewport scopes, raise a separate follow-up task for Jump-To occlusion filtering rather than expanding this one — keep this task to the panel-overlap concern.

 ### Non-goals

-- Do not change Jump-To code generation, matching, or dismiss behavior — only which scopes are enumerated as targets.
-- Do not change the panel layout (it's already a correct dock) or any board layout.
+- Do not change the Jump-To code-generation, matching, or dismiss behavior.
+- Do not make the panel an `absolute`/`fixed` overlay — it must remain a flex dock that pushes the board.
+- Do not change the panel's resize / collapse / persistence behavior.

 ## Acceptance Criteria

-- [ ] With the AI panel open and a board scrolled so a column/card is off-screen (its geometric rect under the panel or outside the visible scroll viewport), that scope gets NO jump pill.
-- [ ] Visible board scopes still get pills; panel scopes still get pills; jumping to a visible target still works.
-- [ ] Triggering Jump-To with the panel open paints no two pills whose rects overlap (no board pill under a panel pill).
-- [ ] No regression: a board that fits (no overflow) labels every scope exactly as before; Jump-To matching/dismiss/flash unchanged.
+- [ ] With the AI panel open at its default width and a board containing cards, the panel container's bounding rect does not horizontally intersect any board card or column scope's bounding rect (every board scope satisfies `scopeRect.right <= panelRect.left`).
+- [ ] Triggering Jump-To with the panel open paints no two pills whose rects overlap; board-scope pills all render left of the panel's left edge and panel-scope pills all render within the panel.
+- [ ] The board content area shrinks to make room for the panel (the panel pushes the board; it never paints over it), at the default width and after a resize.
+- [ ] No regression to panel collapse/expand, width drag/persistence, or Jump-To matching/dismiss.

 ## Tests

-- [ ] Extend a jump-to test (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.browser.test.tsx` or `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/spatial-nav-jump-to.spatial.test.tsx`): with the panel open and a horizontally-scrollable board, assert the off-screen/occluded column scope produces no `[data-jump-code]` pill, and that no two rendered pill rects overlap. Must FAIL before the filter, PASS after.
-- [ ] Keep `ai-panel-dock.spatial.test.tsx` green (dock contract guard).
-- [ ] `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test jump-to-overlay` and `... test ai-panel` green.
-- [ ] Full UI suite green: `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test` (tsc clean).
+- [ ] Browser/spatial test (extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel.spatial.test.tsx` or add a sibling) that renders the production layout (board with ≥1 card + AI panel open), reads `getBoundingClientRect()` for the `[data-testid="ai-panel-container"]` element and for the board card/column focus scopes, and asserts every board scope's `right <= panelRect.left` (no horizontal overlap). This test must FAIL before the fix and PASS after — a true regression guard.
+- [ ] Jump-to regression test (extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.browser.test.tsx` or `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/spatial-nav-jump-to.spatial.test.tsx`): with the panel open, open the overlay and assert no two rendered `[data-jump-code]` pill rects overlap (pairwise rect-intersection check is empty).
+- [ ] `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test ai-panel.spatial` and `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test jump-to-overlay` both green.
+- [ ] Full UI suite green: `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test`.

 ## Workflow

-- Use `/tdd` — write the failing occlusion test first (reproduce a pill landing under the panel), then implement the `useJumpTargets` visibility filter to make it pass.

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -13,37 +13,29 @@

 The original hypothesis (panel overlaps the board) was **wrong**. Measured: the AI panel is already a true non-overlapping right dock — panel left edge = board scroll-viewport right edge (they abut); the panel pushes the board. No layout bug.

-The real cause is in **Jump-To** (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx`). `useJumpTargets` enumerates every `<FocusScope>` in the topmost layer and filters only `rect.width > 0 && rect.height > 0`. On a board wide enough to scroll horizontally, columns scrolled **off-screen** still have a geometric `getBoundingClientRect()` whose `left` lands under the panel (`overflow` clips the *paint*, not the rect). Jump-To paints a pill there → it stacks on the panel's pills. There is no viewport/occlusion check.
-
-### Approach (chosen: fix Jump-To occlusion)
-
-Add visibility filtering to `useJumpTargets` in `jump-to-overlay.tsx`: a scope gets a pill **only if its pill-anchor is actually visible** — inside the viewport, not scrolled out of its nearest scroll container, and not occluded by the panel (or any higher surface). Off-screen / occluded scopes are dropped (you can't jump to a target you can't see — standard vim-sneak/AceJump semantics).
-
-Pick the most robust, deterministic mechanism (e.g. an `elementFromPoint` occlusion test at the pill-anchor point — note the jump-to backdrop is not yet mounted while `useJumpTargets`' effect runs, so the point hits real app DOM — or intersecting each scope rect with its nearest scrolling ancestor's client rect). Keep it panel-agnostic: the board's scroll viewport naturally ends at the panel's left edge, so a viewport/scroll-clip check inherently excludes scopes under the panel.
-
-Keep the dock regression-guard test already added (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel-dock.spatial.test.tsx`) — it pins that the panel stays a non-overlapping dock.
+The real cause is in **Jump-To** (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx`). `useJumpTargets` enumerated every `<FocusScope>` in the topmost layer and filtered only `rect.width > 0 && rect.height > 0`. On a board wide enough to scroll horizontally, columns scrolled **off-screen** still have a geometric `getBoundingClientRect()` whose `left` lands under the panel (`overflow` clips the *paint*, not the rect). Jump-To painted a pill there → it stacked on the panel's pills. No viewport/occlusion check.

-### Non-goals
+### Fix (done)

-- Do not change Jump-To code generation, matching, or dismiss behavior — only which scopes are enumerated as targets.
-- Do not change the panel layout (it's already a correct dock) or any board layout.
+`useJumpTargets` now drops scopes whose pill-anchor is not actually visible, via `isScopeAnchorVisible(fq, rect)`: an `elementFromPoint` hit-test at the exact pill-anchor point (`rect.left + PILL_ANCHOR_OFFSET`, `rect.top + PILL_ANCHOR_OFFSET`) keeps a scope only when the topmost painted element there is the scope's own `data-moniker={fq}` host (or a descendant). One panel-agnostic check covers all three invisibility cases — off-viewport (`null`), scrolled out of a clipping ancestor, and occluded by a higher surface (the panel). The hit-test runs in the mount effect while the overlay body still renders `null`, so the `z-[80]` backdrop is not yet in the DOM. No change to Jump-To code generation, matching, dismiss, or pill rendering (only extracted the shared `PILL_ANCHOR_OFFSET`); no layout change. Dock regression-guard test (`ai-panel-dock.spatial.test.tsx`) kept green.

-- [ ] Visible board scopes still get pills; panel scopes still get pills; jumping to a visible target still works.
-- [ ] Triggering Jump-To with the panel open paints no two pills whose rects overlap (no board pill under a panel pill).
-- [ ] No regression: a board that fits (no overflow) labels every scope exactly as before; Jump-To matching/dismiss/flash unchanged.

+- [x] Visible board scopes still get pills; panel scopes still get pills; jumping to a visible target still works.
+- [x] Triggering Jump-To with the panel open paints no two pills whose rects overlap (no board pill under a panel pill).

Must FAIL before the filter, PASS after.
